title: Kernel lane 2 / critique. Review the new backup fail-closed changes conceptually and identify risks if we roll out a shared exit-code contract to other readiness/peripheral scripts in /Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration. Focus on compatibility risks, false BLOCKED states, and test gaps. Do not e...
summary_text: **Findings** 1. High: shared exit codes are currently dropped by linked-system aggregators, so `75` can become false success upstream. [run-linked-systems.sh:204](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-linked-systems.sh:204) marks any non-zero adapter as `"error"` in JSON, but the script itself does not exit non-zero at end ([run-linked-systems.sh:327](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-linked-systems.sh:327)). [run-local-orchestration.sh:1068](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-local-orchestration.sh:1068) treats runner `rc==0` as linked status `"ok"`. I validated this with a probe adapter exiting `75`: parent rc stayed `0` while result JSON had `exit_code: 75`. 2. High: backup fail-closed semantics are internally inconsistent, which will create compatibility confusion if standardized broadly. Record mode lock contention is explicit `BLOCKED` + `75` ([run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:168](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:168), [run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:170](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:170)), but scan-mode lock contention exits `0` silently ([run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:209](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh:209)). That makes “lock blocked” sometimes retryable-failure and sometimes invisible pass. 3. High: there is already a contract-consumer break in tests for blocked output framing. `check-freee-live.sh` emits JSON on stdout, then emits `BLOCKED ...` on stderr before exiting `75` ([check-freee-live.sh:197](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/check-freee-live.sh:197), [check-freee-live.sh:205](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/check-freee-live.sh:205)). But the blocked-path unit test captures `2>&1` and pipes to `jq` ([test-check-freee-live.sh:112](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-check-freee-live.sh:112), [test-check-freee-live.sh:118](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-check-freee-live.sh:118)), which failed locally with parse error. 4. Medium: false `BLOCKED` risk from broad stderr pattern matching. NotebookLM marks blocked on generic `"Service unavailable"` regex ([check-notebooklm-runtime-readiness.sh:35](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/check-notebooklm-runtime-readiness.sh:35)). freee marks credential-blocked if stderr contains broad tokens including `401|403` ([check-freee-live.sh:125](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/check-freee-live.sh:125)). Google Workspace marks blocked for generic `permission denied` text ([check-googleworkspace-live.sh:109](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/check-googleworkspace-live.sh:109)). These can misclassify local/runtime faults as external blockers. 5. Medium: single-lane fallback can still pass readiness, which is a workflow-violation risk if `BLOCKED` is meant to enforce multi-lane health. `check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh` skips provider probes when live smoke is off ([check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:228](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/scripts/check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:228)). Its tests explicitly accept pass in that mode even when strict probes are otherwise required ([test-check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:100](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:100), [test-check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:110](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-check-kernel-vote-readiness.sh:110)). 6. Medium: test coverage is missing where contract rollout risk is highest. No negative-path test asserts `run-linked-systems.sh` process exit behavior for adapter failure (`75`/`70`) in its dedicated tests ([test-linked-systems-comment-policy.sh:31](/Users/masayuki/Dev/agent-orchestration/tests/test-linked-systems-comment-policy.sh:31), [test-linked-systems-comment-opt-in.sh:38](/Users/m...
